tree_metadata.description: Daily Reflection Tree evaluating Locus, Orientation, and Radius.

nodes.text: Welcome to your evening reflection. Let's take a few minutes to unpack today, not to judge it, but to understand how you showed up. Take a deep breath.
Think about the most frustrating setback or roadblock you faced today. What was your primary internal narrative?

options.label: Why do these things keep happening to me/my project?
This system/process is broken and someone needs to fix it.
What is the immediate next step I can take to bypass this?
How did my previous actions contribute to this roadblock?

nodes.text: When you look at the tasks you actually completed today, how did they get done?

options.label: I pushed through the friction and made them happen.
I got lucky with fewer interruptions than usual.
I followed the schedule and did what was required of me.
I proactively redesigned my workflow to ensure they got done.

nodes.text: An unplanned request or change disrupted your flow today. How did you react?

options.label: I negotiated the priority and adjusted my timelines.
I accepted it, but felt resentful about the changing goalposts.
I paused, assessed the new constraint, and adapted.

nodes.text: It sounds like you held onto your agency today. Even when facing setbacks, you asked '{A1_Q1.answer}'. That's the mindset of a Victor—focusing on the variables within your control.

It sounds like today felt like it happened *to* you. You mentioned feeling '{A1_Q1.answer}'. It's normal to feel constrained, but remember: you always retain the power to choose your response to friction.
Now that we've looked at your sense of control, let's look at where you directed your energy today.
If you had to summarize your primary expectation of your workplace today, what was it?

options.label: To be given clear directions and fair compensation for my time.
To be recognized and appreciated for the hard work I put in.
To find opportunities where my skills could solve existing problems.
To unblock others and move the team's objectives forward.

nodes.text: When you interacted with colleagues today, what was the underlying transaction?

options.label: I was mostly trying to get what I needed from them to finish my work.
I was actively looking for ways to make their jobs slightly easier.
We traded favors—I help them, they help me.

nodes.text: How did you view your 'job description' today?

options.label: As a rigid set of boundaries protecting my time.
As a baseline, from which I looked for places to add unexpected value.
I didn't think about it; I just did whatever was put in front of me.

nodes.text: You operated as a value-creator today. By focusing on '{A2_Q1.answer}', you treated your environment as a place to contribute rather than a place to extract from.

Today's focus seemed a bit transactional. You noted your primary expectation was '{A2_Q1.answer}'. A slight shift toward asking 'What does this situation require of me?' can unlock a lot of latent energy tomorrow.
Finally, let's zoom out and look at the radius of your awareness today.
When you made your most difficult decision today, whose needs were given the most weight?

options.label: My own immediate needs (reducing stress, saving time).
My direct manager's or immediate team's needs.
The overarching goal of the organization or the end customer.

nodes.text: If you had to measure the 'success' of your day, what metric are you using internally right now?

options.label: How much of my personal to-do list I cleared.
How comfortable or stress-free the day was for me.
How much capacity I built in my team or systems.
How much closer the company moved to its strategic goals.

nodes.text: Think about a moment of friction with a colleague today. How did you view them?

options.label: As an obstacle to my personal progress.
As a collaborator who might have different, valid constraints.
I didn't have any friction (working in isolation).

nodes.text: Your radius of concern is wide. Measuring success by '{A3_Q2.answer}' shows that you are thinking systemically, beyond just your own desk.
Your focus today was quite inward. Prioritizing '{A3_Q1.answer}' makes sense when you are overwhelmed, but it can create blind spots in the wider system.
Let's pull all of this together into a final view of your day.

Today, you anchored your mindset in '{A1_Q1.answer}'. Your interactions were driven by the expectation '{A2_Q1.answer}', and your ultimate measure of the day was '{A3_Q2.answer}'. Keep these baselines in mind as you log off. Rest well, and tomorrow, you get to choose your path again.
Session Complete. Thank you for your honesty.
